design_philosophy.core_loop: Wait for opponent's eyes to flash, draw and fire the light gun faster than the AI opponent before they shoot
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Reaction time pressure combined with premature-fire penalty (foul system that makes duel unwinnable if player shoots early)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Quick-draw gunslinger dueling wild west opponents in high-stakes showdowns
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict timing requirements with binary win/loss outcomes per duel; no second chances within a duel (premature fire = automatic loss). Favors reaction speed and discipline over aiming precision. Game rejects button-mashing through foul mechanic—player must wait for explicit signal.

difficulty.lives_system: Players face sequential duels; losing a duel advances to next opponent but accumulation of losses results in defeat
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-duel checkpointing; each duel is discrete and unwinnable if player draws prematurely (foul penalty)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multiple game modes offered (single opponent vs. two opponents, shooting gallery variant), allowing players to choose challenge level. However, within each mode difficulty is fixed.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Game features five distinct gunslinger opponents encountered sequentially in arcade version; NES version includes dual modes and shooting gallery variant
replayability_hooks: Two duel style modes (single vs. dual opponent)
Shooting gallery mode with saloon windows
Attempting to defeat all five opponents in sequence
Speed running/high-score chasing via reaction time optimization

world_building_techniques: Wild West setting with saloon and gunslinger imagery
Sprite-based opponents with distinct visual personalities
Shooting gallery environment with saloon windows as context

boss_design.philosophy: Each gunslinger opponent is functionally equivalent—all follow same pattern (eyes flash, then draw and fire). Difficulty scales through opponent variety and dual-opponent mode rather than individual boss complexity.

faction_indicators: Player (gunslinger protagonist) vs. outlaws/wild gunmen (5 opponents)
Implicit law-vs-outlaw narrative through western setting
danger_signals: Opponent's eyes flashing as signal to draw (game explicitly uses eye contact as threat indicator)
Saloon shooting gallery with targets appearing unexpectedly from windows
environmental_storytelling: Saloon setting establishes western frontier context
Gunslinger character sprites convey personality through visual design
Holster belt peripheral accessory reinforces gunslinger fantasy
Funeral march music plays on player defeat, signaling narrative stakes

sources_used: Wikipedia - Wild Gunman article (gameplay mechanics, modes, arcade/NES versions, design credits, foul system, five opponents, Zapper peripheral details)
